description_long: This stone monastery features a central cylindrical tower with a conical, shingled roof, rising above a complex of interconnected rectangular buildings. The structure is constructed from weathered, gray-brown stone blocks, giving it a robust and ancient appearance. The main entrance is a large arched doorway, and several smaller windows, some arched, are distributed across the facades. The roofs are steeply pitched and covered with overlapping stone or slate tiles, adding to the medieval character. The building sits on a raised stone foundation supported by visible columns or piers. The overall design is compact and fortified, typical of Romanesque monastic architecture.
description: A weathered stone monastery with a central cylindrical tower, steep shingled roofs, and arched doorways and windows, exuding a fortified medieval character.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main arched entrance, central tower, and symmetrical wings with small windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical tower, steep rooflines, and a smaller attached structure to the left.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a secondary arched entrance, more windows, and the full height of the central tower.
description_view_3: Opposite front view with a prominent arched doorway and a clear view of the attached side building.
